title: Double typhoon approaches Japan bringing torrential rains
summary: Typhoon No. 7 approaches the Tokai and Kanto regions, while the alert remains for the risk of landslides and floods.

title: Strong earthquake shakes the Fuji Five Lakes region in Yamanashi
summary: A magnitude 5.6 earthquake shakes central Japan, unrelated to Mount Fuji, but increasing the risk of landslides ahead of the typhoon.

title: Japan prepares for crucial match against Brazil in the World Cup
summary: The Japanese national team fine-tunes details for the decisive match against the five-time world champions, with Tomiyasu, Maeda, and Nagatomo as key figures.

title: U-20 World Cup: France and Spain advance, Cape Verde makes history
summary: The group stage concludes with France and Spain advancing, while debutant Cape Verde achieves an unprecedented qualification to the round of 16.

title: Japanese political agenda debates defense, constitution, and imperial succession
summary: The Japanese government faces debates on the revision of non-nuclear principles and the national flag law, as well as imperial succession and police reorganization.

title: Japan's cultural and social life shows its diversity
summary: From theater and music to vinyl collecting and the growing popularity of onigiri in France, Japanese culture is constantly renewing itself.

title: Movements in the Japanese economy and corporate infrastructure
summary: The Japanese business landscape is being reconfigured with changes at the top of Takeda, NTT's expansion into solar energy, and KDDI's restructuring.

title: Japanese multi-sport: rugby, boxing, shogi, and school athletics
summary: Sports shine on multiple fronts, from Fujii's shogi promise to Usyk's boxing, rugby clinics, and summer baseball in schools.

title: US-Iran conflict intensifies with mutual bombings
summary: The US bombs Iranian missile installations in response to attacks on ships, while Trump denounces a violation of the ceasefire agreement.

title: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on countries that tax tech companies
summary: The former US president promises trade retaliation against France and other nations for the tax on digital services of big tech companies.

title: US government authorizes resumption of access to AI model 'Mythos'
summary: The administration allows Anthropic and OpenAI to offer their most powerful artificial intelligence systems to a select group of companies, reigniting the debate on government control.

title: Landslides and floods hit western Japan due to typhoon rains
summary: Heavy rains wreak havoc in Yamaguchi, Hiroshima, and Fukuoka, with a deadly landslide, flooded homes, and a large sinkhole on a public road.

title: Multiple traffic accidents and tragic incidents in Japan
summary: Serious accidents are recorded in various parts of the country, including vehicles falling into the sea and into voids, leaving fatalities and injured.

title: Tensions and alliances on the international stage
summary: From peace agreements between Israel and Lebanon to Burkina Faso's break with France and the Ebola alert, global diplomacy faces multiple crises.

title: Health and welfare: Japan faces heat, rare diseases, and disability
summary: Authorities seek to reduce heatstroke deaths, while rare diseases like Cockayne syndrome are made visible and care for the disabled is debated.

title: Day of ups and downs for Japanese players in MLB and NPB
summary: Ohtani gets a hit, Sasaki suffers his fifth loss, Okamoto hits his 19th home run, and the Angels fire their general manager due to the team's poor results.

title: Earthquake devastation in Venezuela leaves more than 900 dead
summary: The death toll rises to 920 dead and more than 3,300 injured, while rescue teams struggle against time and resource shortages.

title: Hakone plans to implement a 350 yen accommodation tax
summary: The tourist city, which receives 20 million visitors a year, aims to introduce the first ordinary accommodation tax in Japan by 2028.

title: Japan expands language test dates for foreign workers
summary: Faced with the increase in demand for foreign labor, the JLPT offering will be expanded, while the challenges of social integration of immigrants persist.

title: Science and environment: progress against CO2 and climate alarm
summary: Tokyo researchers develop a technique to convert CO2 into fuel, while Europe suffers a historic heatwave and the albatross recovers in Japan.
